text: The inferior pulmonary ligament is identified and divided to the level of inferior pulmonary vein and its associated lymph nodes are resected.
Next, the right main bronchus is identified.
The mediastinal pleura between the esophagus and the right main bronchus is cut.
Proceeding cranially, the mediastinal pleura is cut up to the carina.
All the lymph nodes are kept with the esophagus.
Here, we have to preserve two structures.
the bronchial artery and the vagal branches to the bronchus and lung which helps in post-op recovery.
Proceeding cranially, the carina is identified and all the subcarinal lymph nodes are removed with the esophagus.
Precaution is taken here to see that no lymph nodes are left behind in the subcarinal region.
Proceeding on the left side, the left main bronchus is identified.
Here we can see this is the left main bronchus and all the lymph nodes are taken into the specimen.
The left main bronchus is completely dissected.
The azagus vein is identified and the medicinal flora is cut close to the azagus vein.
Once the azagus vein is separated, aorta is identified.
Cutting in the ovascular plane and the direct branches from aorta to the esophagus
All the lymph nodes are included into the specimen.
Once the dissection of azygous vein and aorta is completed, the dissection proceeds cranially.
Here the left main bronchus is identified from the posterior part.
Both the dissection anterior and posterior are joined completely separating the esophagus from the
bronchus, and carina.
Staying in the avascular plane between the pericardium and the esophagus, mobilization of the middle esophagus continues.
Here, the esophagus is separated from the heart, keeping all the lymph nodes and the fibrofibrity tissue with the esophagus.
Once the mobilization of the middle esophagus is completed, the dissection proceeds distally.
Here the mediastinal pleura is cut and dissection is done between the aorta and esophagus.
Keeping all the lymph node and fibro fatty tissue with the esophagus, plane is dissected between the aorta posteriorly and esophagus anteriorly.
For giving traction on the esophagus, we use gauze piece.
The gauze piece is wrapped around the esophagus and traction is given on the esophagus with the help of gauze piece.
The contraction is provided by the mediastinal pleura.
In this case, the thoracic duct is identified and preserved.
In other cases, we may clip the thoracic duct.
Proceeding digitally, the lower esophagus is completed, separated posteriorly up to the hiatus.
Precaution is taken here not to open the hiatus, otherwise the gas will leak into the abdomen.
Next, anterior dissection of the lower esophagus is completed by separating the esophagus from the pericardium.
At this stage, the mobilization of the middle and lower esophagus is complete and the mobilization of the supra-asagus part of the esophagus starts.
Supra-asagus pleura is cut and the asagus vein is mobilized completely.
With the help of hemolog clip applied on both the sides of the asagus vein,
The isagus vein is cut.
Few surgeons prefer not to cut the isagus vein and complete the esophageal mobilization preserving the isagus vein.
Complete cut of the mediastinal pleura is given in the supra-izagus part up to the thoracic inlet.
Mobilization of the upper one third of the esophagus is done keeping all the fibro fatty tissue with lymph nodes with the esophagus.
The esophagus is separated from the trachea taking precaution not to injure the trachea.
Mobilization of the esophagus is completed once the thoracic inlet is reached.
This is the final appearance once the mobilization is done.
This is the mobilized esophagus.
This is the pulmonary vein.
This is the pericardium here.
Right main bronchus, carina and left main bronchus going cranially.
This is the trachea.
Moving caudally, this is the vertebra here and we can see behind the aorta and thoracic duct.
This is the diaphragm.
This is the hiatus and the lung.
This completes the thoracic component of esophagectomy.
Intracostal drainage tube is placed and port closure is done.
